logic_depth_estimate.worst_path: state_reg -> aes_key_expansion (40 XOR chain + 10 S-Box lookups for round 10) -> aes_round_logic (16 S-Box parallel + ShiftRows wiring + MixColumns xtime+XOR + AddRoundKey XOR) -> state_next -> register D

functional_gaps.description: Uses 40 S-Box instances (10 rounds x 4 per SubWord) in generate loop instead of planned 4 shared instances. This is architecturally correct for pure combinational on-the-fly key expansion but increases LUT usage. Total S-Box count across design: 56 (40 in key_expansion + 16 in round_logic).
functional_gaps.impact: Resource usage ~3810 LUTs vs budget of 4000 — tight but fits.

functional_gaps.description: Register declarations use Verilog-2001 initial values (e.g., 'reg rst_n_meta = 1\'b0'). These are well-supported for FPGA synthesis (map to INIT attributes) but may not be portable to ASIC flows.

recommendation: Resource usage is tight at ~3810/4000 LUTs. If synthesis exceeds the budget, consider trading key_expansion combinational chain latency for a sequential approach (compute round key over 2 cycles per round), which would reduce S-Box instances from 40 to 4.
